key_insight: PA-SciML makes agentic scientific machine learning verification-first by auditing candidate surrogates against explicit physics checks, not just error metrics.
engineering_relevance: For solid mechanics surrogate modeling, this helps ensure discovered models respect boundary conditions, scaling, superposition, and causality before they are trusted in design or simulation workflows.
method: Fix the evaluator before search and define machine-checkable physics requirements.
Check each trained candidate directly on predicted outputs, while separately probing input/load-history spans for high-violation cases.
Optionally run advisory numerical probes and isolate one modeling change at a time to track which edits improve score.
evidence_signal: In the reported elasticity example, the selected model had lower validation error than the error-only baseline, and in elastodynamics an error-only baseline with similar mean error failed a causality check while the PA-SciML model passed.
watch_item: Physics audits are only as strong as the chosen checks and search ranges, so incomplete requirements could still miss important failure modes.
paper_title: Physics-Audited Agentic Discovery in Scientific Machine Learning
